_meta.source: Conventional scholarly dating; Conventional scholarly dating. Pre-Abrahamic dates are highly approximate / traditional Ussher-adjacent; patriarchal era is debated (Middle Bronze placement used here); monarchic era follows Thiele; NT/early-church dates follow standard consensus.
_meta.license: curated dataset, redistributed with the app
_meta.contested_policy: pre-Abrahamic and patriarchal dates are traditional/approximate and flagged as such

events.title: Creation
events.summary: God creates the heavens and the earth in six days and rests on the seventh.

events.title: The Fall
events.summary: Adam and Eve disobey, are exiled from Eden, and the curse enters creation.

events.title: Cain Kills Abel
events.summary: The first murder: Cain slays his brother Abel out of jealousy.

events.title: Birth of Seth
events.summary: Seth is born as the appointed son through whom the godly line continues.

events.title: Enoch Walks With God
events.summary: Enoch walks with God and is taken without seeing death.

events.title: Methuselah
events.summary: Methuselah, the longest-lived patriarch (969 years), bridges Enoch and Noah.

events.title: The Flood
events.summary: God floods the earth; Noah, his family, and the animals are saved in the ark.

events.title: Noahic Covenant
events.summary: God promises never again to destroy the earth by flood; the rainbow is the sign.

events.title: Tower of Babel
events.summary: Humanity's united tongue is confused and the peoples scattered across the earth.

events.title: Call of Abram
events.summary: God calls Abram from Haran to Canaan with a promise of land, seed, and blessing.

events.title: Abram in Egypt
events.summary: Famine drives Abram to Egypt, where he passes Sarai off as his sister.

events.title: Abram and Lot Separate
events.summary: Abram gives Lot first choice of land; Lot settles near Sodom.

events.title: Melchizedek Blesses Abram
events.summary: The priest-king of Salem blesses Abram and receives a tithe.

events.title: Abrahamic Covenant
events.summary: God ratifies an everlasting covenant with Abraham: land, seed, and worldwide blessing.

events.title: Birth of Ishmael
events.summary: Hagar bears Ishmael to Abraham at Sarai's urging.

events.title: Destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah
events.summary: Fire and sulfur destroy the cities of the plain; Lot and his daughters escape.

events.title: Birth of Isaac
events.summary: Sarah bears Isaac, the son of promise, in her old age.

events.title: Binding of Isaac (Akedah)
events.summary: God tests Abraham, who offers Isaac on Mount Moriah; a ram is provided.

events.title: Isaac Marries Rebekah
events.summary: Abraham's servant brings Rebekah from Mesopotamia as Isaac's bride.

events.title: Birth of Jacob and Esau
events.summary: Twins are born to Rebekah; the older will serve the younger.

events.title: Esau Sells the Birthright
events.summary: Esau trades his birthright to Jacob for a bowl of stew.

events.title: Jacob's Ladder
events.summary: Fleeing Esau, Jacob dreams of a stairway to heaven and receives God's promise.

events.title: Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel
events.summary: Jacob serves Laban fourteen years to marry both sisters.

events.title: Birth of the Twelve Tribes
events.summary: Jacob's twelve sons, fathers of Israel's tribes, are born to four mothers.

events.title: Jacob Wrestles With God
events.summary: At Peniel Jacob wrestles a divine figure and is renamed Israel.

events.title: Joseph Sold Into Slavery
events.summary: Joseph's brothers sell him to traders bound for Egypt.

events.title: Joseph in Pharaoh's Prison
events.summary: Falsely accused by Potiphar's wife, Joseph is imprisoned and interprets dreams.

events.title: Joseph Rises to Power
events.summary: Joseph interprets Pharaoh's dreams and is set over all Egypt.

events.title: Israel Enters Egypt
events.summary: Jacob's family of seventy settles in Goshen during the famine.

events.title: Death of Joseph
events.summary: Joseph dies in Egypt, asking that his bones be carried to the promised land.

events.title: Israel Enslaved in Egypt
events.summary: A new pharaoh, who knew not Joseph, enslaves the Israelites with hard labor.

events.title: Birth of Moses
events.summary: Moses is born to Levite parents and rescued by Pharaoh's daughter.

events.title: Moses Flees to Midian
events.summary: After killing an Egyptian, Moses flees to Midian and marries Zipporah.

events.title: The Burning Bush
events.summary: At Horeb God calls Moses from a flaming bush and reveals the name YHWH.

events.title: The Ten Plagues
events.summary: YHWH judges Egypt with ten plagues, climaxing in the death of the firstborn.

events.title: First Passover
events.summary: Israel marks their doorposts with lamb's blood; the destroyer passes over.

events.title: The Exodus
events.summary: Israel leaves Egypt; the sea splits and Pharaoh's army drowns.

events.title: Manna in the Wilderness
events.summary: God feeds Israel daily with manna for forty years.

events.title: Covenant at Sinai
events.summary: YHWH descends in fire on Sinai and gives the Ten Commandments.

events.title: Golden Calf
events.summary: Israel makes and worships a golden calf while Moses is on the mountain.

events.title: Tabernacle Built
events.summary: The portable sanctuary is constructed; God's glory fills it.

events.title: Twelve Spies
events.summary: Ten spies bring an evil report; Israel is sentenced to forty years' wandering.

events.title: Korah's Rebellion
events.summary: The earth swallows Korah and his followers for rebelling against Moses.

events.title: Bronze Serpent
events.summary: Moses lifts up a bronze serpent; those who look on it are healed.

events.title: Balaam's Oracles
events.summary: Hired to curse Israel, the prophet Balaam blesses them four times instead.

events.title: Death of Moses
events.summary: Moses views Canaan from Mount Nebo and dies; God buries him.

events.title: Crossing the Jordan
events.summary: The Jordan piles in a heap as Israel crosses on dry ground into Canaan.

events.title: Fall of Jericho
events.summary: After seven days of marching and a great shout, Jericho's walls fall.

events.title: Battle of Ai
events.summary: After Achan's sin is purged, Israel takes Ai by ambush.

events.title: Sun Stands Still
events.summary: At Gibeon Joshua commands the sun to stand still until victory is complete.

events.title: Land Divided Among the Tribes
events.summary: Joshua apportions Canaan among the twelve tribes by lot at Shiloh.

events.title: Death of Joshua
events.summary: Joshua renews the covenant at Shechem and dies at 110.

events.title: Othniel Delivers Israel
events.summary: The first judge frees Israel from Cushan-Rishathaim of Aram.

events.title: Ehud and Eglon
events.summary: Left-handed Ehud assassinates the Moabite king Eglon and frees Israel.

events.title: Deborah and Barak
events.summary: The prophet-judge Deborah leads Israel; Jael kills the fleeing Sisera.

events.title: Gideon and the 300
events.summary: With only 300 men Gideon routs the vast Midianite army by night.

events.title: Jephthah's Vow
events.summary: The Gileadite judge defeats Ammon but loses his daughter to a rash vow.

events.title: Samson the Nazirite
events.summary: Samson judges Israel against the Philistines until betrayed by Delilah.

events.title: Ruth and Boaz
events.summary: A Moabite widow joins Israel and becomes great-grandmother of David.

events.title: Birth of Samuel
events.summary: Hannah's prayer is answered; she dedicates Samuel to the Lord at Shiloh.

events.title: Ark Captured by Philistines
events.summary: Israel loses the ark in battle; the Philistines return it after plagues.

events.title: Saul Anointed King
events.summary: Samuel anoints Saul as Israel's first king at the people's demand.

events.title: David Anointed
events.summary: Samuel secretly anoints the shepherd-boy David as the next king.

events.title: David and Goliath
events.summary: Young David fells the Philistine champion Goliath with a sling.

events.title: Death of Saul
events.summary: Saul and Jonathan fall on Mount Gilboa to the Philistines.

events.title: David Becomes King
events.summary: David reigns over Judah at Hebron, then over all Israel at Jerusalem.

events.title: Jerusalem Captured
events.summary: David takes the Jebusite stronghold and makes it the City of David.

events.title: Ark Brought to Jerusalem
events.summary: David dances before the ark as it enters Jerusalem.

events.title: Davidic Covenant
events.summary: God promises David an everlasting throne through his offspring.

events.title: David and Bathsheba
events.summary: David's adultery and murder are exposed by the prophet Nathan.

events.title: Absalom's Revolt
events.summary: David's son Absalom rebels and is killed in the forest of Ephraim.

events.title: Solomon Crowned
events.summary: On David's deathbed Solomon is anointed king over Israel.

events.title: Solomon's Wisdom
events.summary: At Gibeon Solomon asks for wisdom and is granted it along with wealth and honor.

events.title: First Temple Built
events.summary: Solomon builds the temple in seven years; glory fills it at dedication.

events.title: Queen of Sheba Visits
events.summary: The queen of Sheba tests Solomon's wisdom and is astonished.

events.title: Kingdom Divided
events.summary: Ten tribes secede under Jeroboam; Judah and Benjamin keep Rehoboam.

events.title: Shishak Sacks Jerusalem
events.summary: Pharaoh Shishak plunders the temple treasures.

events.title: Elijah Announces Drought
events.summary: Elijah declares a three-year drought against Ahab's Baal-worship.

events.title: Elijah on Mount Carmel
events.summary: Fire from heaven consumes Elijah's offering; the prophets of Baal are slain.

events.title: Elijah Taken Up
events.summary: A chariot of fire carries Elijah to heaven; Elisha receives a double portion.

events.title: Elisha Heals Naaman
events.summary: The Aramean commander Naaman is cleansed of leprosy in the Jordan.

events.title: Jehu's Purge
events.summary: Jehu kills Joram, Ahaziah, and Jezebel and wipes out Baal-worship.

events.title: Amos Prophesies
events.summary: The shepherd of Tekoa denounces Israel's social injustice.

events.title: Hosea's Marriage
events.summary: God commands Hosea to marry an unfaithful wife as a sign to Israel.

events.title: Jonah at Nineveh
events.summary: Reluctant Jonah preaches; Nineveh repents and is spared.

events.title: Call of Isaiah
events.summary: In the year King Uzziah died, Isaiah sees the LORD high and lifted up.

events.title: Fall of Samaria
events.summary: Assyria destroys the northern kingdom and exiles the ten tribes.

events.title: Hezekiah's Reforms
events.summary: Hezekiah purges idolatry and trusts in YHWH.

events.title: Sennacherib Besieges Jerusalem
events.summary: An angel of the LORD strikes 185,000 Assyrians; Jerusalem is delivered.

events.title: Reign of Manasseh
events.summary: Judah's longest, most wicked reign — later repented of in Babylon.

events.title: Josiah's Reform
events.summary: The Book of the Law is found in the temple; Josiah leads sweeping reform.

events.title: Call of Jeremiah
events.summary: Jeremiah is appointed prophet to the nations from the womb.

events.title: Fall of Nineveh
events.summary: Babylonians and Medes destroy Assyria's capital, fulfilling Nahum.

events.title: Battle of Carchemish
events.summary: Nebuchadnezzar crushes Egypt; Babylon takes the world stage.

events.title: First Deportation to Babylon
events.summary: Daniel and other young nobles are taken to Babylon.

events.title: Call of Ezekiel
events.summary: By the river Chebar Ezekiel sees the chariot-throne of God.

events.title: Fall of Jerusalem
events.summary: Nebuchadnezzar destroys the temple and city; Judah is exiled.

events.title: Three Hebrews in the Furnace
events.summary: Three friends survive the fiery furnace; a fourth like a son of the gods appears.

events.title: Daniel in the Lions' Den
events.summary: Daniel is preserved overnight among lions for his prayer to God.

events.title: Writing on the Wall
events.summary: A hand writes mene, mene, tekel, parsin; Babylon falls that night.

events.title: Babylon Falls to Persia
events.summary: Cyrus the Persian captures Babylon, ending the empire.

events.title: Decree of Cyrus
events.summary: Cyrus permits the exiles to return and rebuild the temple.

events.title: First Return Under Zerubbabel
events.summary: About fifty thousand Jews return with Zerubbabel and the priest Jeshua.

events.title: Altar Rebuilt
events.summary: The returned exiles raise the altar and resume sacrifices.

events.title: Haggai and Zechariah Prophesy
events.summary: The prophets stir the people to finish the temple.

events.title: Second Temple Completed
events.summary: The second temple is dedicated, twenty years after the foundation was laid.

events.title: Esther Saves Her People
events.summary: Queen Esther exposes Haman's plot; the Jews are spared and Purim begins.

events.title: Ezra Returns
events.summary: The priest-scribe Ezra leads a second wave of exiles back and teaches the Law.

events.title: Nehemiah Rebuilds the Walls
events.summary: Nehemiah rebuilds Jerusalem's walls in fifty-two days despite opposition.

events.title: Malachi Prophesies
events.summary: The last OT prophet warns of formal worship and promises a coming messenger.

events.title: Alexander the Great
events.summary: Greek conquest of the Near East fulfills Daniel's vision of the he-goat.

events.title: Septuagint Begun
events.summary: Greek translation of the Hebrew scriptures begins in Alexandria.

events.title: Maccabean Revolt
events.summary: Judah Maccabee rebels against Antiochus IV's desecration of the temple.

events.title: Rededication of the Temple
events.summary: The temple is cleansed and rededicated, giving rise to Hanukkah.

events.title: Hasmonean Kingdom
events.summary: An independent Jewish state under the Hasmonean priest-kings.

events.title: Pompey Takes Jerusalem
events.summary: Rome annexes Judea; Pompey enters the Holy of Holies.

events.title: Herod the Great Begins Reign
events.summary: Rome installs Herod as client king of Judea.

events.title: Herod's Temple Begun
events.summary: Herod begins a massive expansion of the second temple complex.

events.title: Annunciation to Mary
events.summary: Gabriel announces to Mary that she will bear the Son of the Most High.

events.title: Birth of John the Baptist
events.summary: Elizabeth bears John in her old age; Zechariah's tongue is loosed.

events.title: Birth of Jesus
events.summary: Jesus is born in Bethlehem; shepherds and angels gather.

events.title: Visit of the Magi
events.summary: Wise men from the east bring gold, frankincense, and myrrh.

events.title: Flight to Egypt
events.summary: Warned in a dream, Joseph takes Mary and Jesus to Egypt.

events.title: Jesus in the Temple
events.summary: At twelve Jesus astonishes the teachers in his Father's house.

events.title: Ministry of John the Baptist
events.summary: John preaches repentance and baptizes in the Jordan.

events.title: Baptism of Jesus
events.summary: The Spirit descends as a dove; the Father speaks.

events.title: Temptation in the Wilderness
events.summary: Forty days of fasting; Jesus rebuffs Satan with scripture.

events.title: Water Into Wine
events.summary: Jesus' first sign: water becomes wine at a wedding in Cana.

events.title: Calling of the Twelve
events.summary: Jesus appoints twelve apostles to be with him and to be sent out.

events.title: Sermon on the Mount
events.summary: Jesus delivers the Beatitudes and the charter of the kingdom.

events.title: Feeding of the 5,000
events.summary: Five loaves and two fish feed five thousand men.

events.title: Jesus Walks on Water
events.summary: Jesus comes to the disciples on the sea; Peter walks briefly to him.

events.title: Transfiguration
events.summary: Jesus shines like the sun; Moses and Elijah appear.

events.title: Raising of Lazarus
events.summary: Jesus calls Lazarus from the tomb after four days.

events.title: Triumphal Entry
events.summary: Jesus rides a donkey into Jerusalem to cries of Hosanna.

events.title: Last Supper
events.summary: Jesus institutes the new covenant in his blood at Passover.

events.title: Agony in Gethsemane
events.summary: Jesus prays in anguish; his sweat is like drops of blood.

events.title: Crucifixion
events.summary: Jesus is crucified, dies, and is buried.

events.title: Resurrection
events.summary: Jesus rises from the dead on the third day.

events.title: Ascension
events.summary: Forty days after the resurrection, Jesus ascends from the Mount of Olives.

events.title: Pentecost
events.summary: The Spirit falls; Peter preaches; three thousand are baptized.

events.title: Ananias and Sapphira
events.summary: A couple is struck dead for lying to the Holy Spirit.

events.title: Martyrdom of Stephen
events.summary: The first Christian martyr is stoned as Saul looks on.

events.title: Conversion of Saul
events.summary: On the road to Damascus, Saul of Tarsus meets the risen Christ.

events.title: Cornelius and the Gentiles
events.summary: Peter is sent to the Roman centurion; the Spirit falls on Gentiles.

events.title: Disciples Called Christians
events.summary: In Antioch the followers of Jesus are first called Christians.

events.title: Martyrdom of James (son of Zebedee)
events.summary: Herod Agrippa kills James with the sword.

events.title: Peter Freed From Prison
events.summary: An angel leads Peter out of Herod's prison.

events.title: Paul's First Missionary Journey
events.summary: Paul and Barnabas evangelize Cyprus and southern Galatia.

events.title: Epistle of James Written
events.summary: James, the Lord's brother, writes the earliest surviving NT letter.

events.title: Council of Jerusalem
events.summary: The apostles decide Gentile converts need not be circumcised.

events.title: Galatians Written
events.summary: Paul defends justification by faith apart from the works of the Law.

events.title: Paul's Second Missionary Journey
events.summary: Paul carries the gospel into Macedonia and Greece.

events.title: Philippian Jailer Saved
events.summary: An earthquake frees Paul and Silas; the jailer believes.

events.title: 1–2 Thessalonians Written
events.summary: Paul's earliest preserved letters to a church, on Christ's return.

events.title: Paul's Third Missionary Journey
events.summary: Three-year ministry centered at Ephesus.

events.title: 1 Corinthians Written
events.summary: Paul addresses divisions, immorality, and resurrection at Corinth.

events.title: Romans Written
events.summary: Paul's most systematic exposition of the gospel.

events.title: Paul Arrested in Jerusalem
events.summary: A temple riot leads to Paul's arrest and Roman custody.

events.title: Imprisonment at Caesarea
events.summary: Two years in Caesarean custody under Felix and Festus.

events.title: Paul's Shipwreck
events.summary: En route to Rome, Paul is shipwrecked at Malta and all are saved.

events.title: Paul's First Roman Imprisonment
events.summary: Two years under house arrest in Rome, preaching freely.

events.title: Prison Epistles Written
events.summary: Ephesians, Philippians, Colossians, and Philemon written from custody.

events.title: Gospel of Mark Written
events.summary: The earliest gospel, traditionally drawn from Peter's preaching.

events.title: Gospel of Matthew Written
events.summary: Jewish-Christian gospel framing Jesus as the new Moses and son of David.

events.title: Luke–Acts Written
events.summary: Luke's two-volume history of Jesus and the early church to Theophilus.

events.title: Martyrdom of James the Just
events.summary: James, brother of Jesus and leader of the Jerusalem church, is killed by the Sanhedrin.

events.title: Pastoral Epistles Written
events.summary: Letters to Timothy and Titus on church order and faithful ministry.

events.title: Great Fire of Rome
events.summary: Nero blames Christians; the first imperial persecution begins.

events.title: Martyrdom of Peter
events.summary: Peter is crucified in Rome, traditionally head-downward.

events.title: Martyrdom of Paul
events.summary: Paul is beheaded on the Ostian Way under Nero.

events.title: First Jewish–Roman War
events.summary: Jewish revolt against Rome ends in catastrophe.

events.title: Destruction of the Second Temple
events.summary: Titus razes Jerusalem and burns the temple, fulfilling Jesus' prophecy.

events.title: Fall of Masada
events.summary: The last Jewish holdouts kill themselves rather than surrender.

events.title: Hebrews Written
events.summary: An anonymous treatise presenting Christ as a greater high priest.

events.title: General Epistles Written
events.summary: 1–2 Peter, 1–3 John, and Jude — letters to scattered churches.

events.title: John Exiled to Patmos
events.summary: Under Domitian the apostle John is exiled to the island of Patmos.

events.title: Revelation Written
events.summary: John records his apocalyptic vision of Christ, the churches, and the end.

events.title: Gospel of John Written
events.summary: The fourth gospel, framing Jesus as the eternal Logos.

events.title: Death of the Apostle John
events.summary: John dies at Ephesus, the last surviving apostle.

events.title: Didache Composed
events.summary: Early Christian church manual on the Two Ways, baptism, and Eucharist.

events.title: Martyrdom of Ignatius
events.summary: The bishop of Antioch is fed to beasts in Rome under Trajan.

events.title: Bar Kochba Revolt
events.summary: The second Jewish revolt against Rome ends in expulsion from Jerusalem.

events.title: Martyrdom of Polycarp
events.summary: Polycarp, disciple of John, is burned and stabbed at Smyrna.

events.title: Marcion's Canon
events.summary: Marcion proposes a truncated canon, prompting the church to define its own.

events.title: Martyrdom of Justin
events.summary: The first major Christian apologist is beheaded in Rome.

events.title: Irenaeus Writes Against Heresies
events.summary: Irenaeus defends apostolic teaching against Gnosticism.

events.title: Martyrdom of Perpetua and Felicity
events.summary: Young noblewoman and her slave are killed in the arena at Carthage.

events.title: Origen Writes On First Principles
events.summary: Foundational systematic theology of the Eastern church.

events.title: Diocletian Persecution
events.summary: The empire's most severe persecution of Christians.

events.title: Battle of the Milvian Bridge
events.summary: Constantine wins under the sign of the cross.

events.title: Edict of Milan
events.summary: Constantine and Licinius legalize Christianity throughout the empire.

events.title: Council of Nicaea
events.summary: Defines the Son as homoousios with the Father; the Nicene Creed is drafted.

events.title: Athanasius' Festal Letter
events.summary: First listing of the 27 NT books as we have them today.

events.title: First Council of Constantinople
events.summary: Affirms the deity of the Holy Spirit; finalizes the Nicene-Constantinopolitan Creed.

events.title: Jerome's Vulgate
events.summary: Jerome's Latin translation becomes the Bible of the Western church.

events.title: Augustine's Confessions
events.summary: Augustine's spiritual autobiography and prayer.

events.title: Council of Carthage Affirms Canon
events.summary: African councils confirm the 27-book NT canon for Western use.

events.title: Sack of Rome
events.summary: The Visigoths sack Rome, prompting Augustine to write City of God.

events.title: Augustine's City of God
events.summary: Augustine's monumental defense of Christianity after Rome's fall.

events.title: Council of Ephesus
events.summary: Affirms Mary as Theotokos; condemns Nestorius.

events.title: Council of Chalcedon
events.summary: Defines Christ as one person in two natures, without confusion or division.

events.title: Second Council of Constantinople
events.summary: Condemns the Three Chapters and clarifies Chalcedonian Christology.

events.title: Gregory the Great Becomes Pope
events.summary: Gregory's papacy shapes medieval Western Christianity.

events.title: Third Council of Constantinople
events.summary: Affirms Christ has two wills, divine and human.

events.title: Second Council of Nicaea
events.summary: Restores veneration of icons after the iconoclast controversy.
